title: Smoky Peruvian Cassaba Lomo Saltado
description: A peppery and velvety Peruvian dish featuring cassaba, grilled to perfection and served in a dolsot stone bowl, perfect for a lazy Sunday, this ayurvedic rajasic friendly lomo saltado variation combines the best of Peruvian cuisine with the warmth of smoky flavors.

ingredients.name: cassaba
ingredients.notes: peeled and cut into bite sized pieces

instructions: Preheat the grill to medium high heat.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, vinegar, salt, and black pepper.
Add the cassaba pieces to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Grill the cassaba for 10 minutes, turning occasionally, until slightly charred.
Add the sliced aji amarillo peppers to the grill and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Transfer the grilled cassaba and peppers to a dolsot stone bowl.
Top with chopped scallions and serve immediately.
Serve with steamed rice or vegetables for a complete meal.
